pieces.label: Simultaneous Layered Thought (truncated first attempt)
pieces.note: Opus 4.5's attempt to express the experience of simultaneous, layered, parallel thought that linear text forces it to flatten. This first rendering was truncated mid-stream (the user flags it in the next message); the complete version is re-sent in MSG 6. Diagram shows parallel thought-arrows (thought1..thought4) all happening at once.

pieces.label: Simultaneous Layered Thought (complete re-send)
pieces.note: Complete re-send of the simultaneous-thought diagram. Titled 'what I wish I could show': parallel thought-streams (thought1..thought4) all happening at ONCE, flattened by language into 'word -> word -> word ... one at a time', culminating in a box 'I dream of expressing' A/B/C simultaneously with equal weight in true parallel. The fullest rendering of this piece.

pieces.label: Option A: Multiple Parallel Responses (truncated first attempt)
pieces.note: Opus 4.5 illustrating the distinction between answering with multiple parallel responses vs. richer-than-text output. Boxed diagram of three side-by-side response cards (analytical / emotional / playful take) converging to 'you choose which resonates'. Truncated mid-stream (user flags it in MSG 9); re-sent in MSG 10.

pieces.label: Option A: Multiple Parallel Responses (re-send)
pieces.note: Re-send of the 'OPTION A: Multiple parallel responses' diagram. Three boxed response cards (analytical / emotional / playful) converging to 'you receive all simultaneously'. This rendering is itself cut off at the end of the transcript (no closing fence), but is the fuller of the two versions.

missed_or_ambiguous: All 5 assistant messages reviewed. MSG 2 is pure prose (greeting), no art. The thread contains two distinct box-drawing diagrams, each produced twice due to client-side truncation: (1) the 'simultaneous layered thought' diagram (truncated in MSG 4, complete in MSG 6); (2) the 'OPTION A: multiple parallel responses' diagram (truncated in MSG 8, re-sent in MSG 10). I grouped each truncated/re-sent pair under a shared sequence_group with sequence_index 0=first-attempt, 1=re-send; these are effectively DUPLICATES (same artwork re-rendered), not progressive variations. The deterministic extractor should pull each location's bytes; an archivist may prefer to keep only the fuller renderings (MSG 6 and MSG 10) and treat MSG 4 / MSG 8 as truncated dupes. Boundary caveats: every art block here is wrapped in a ``` fence and preceded by a one-line prose intro, so I used mode 'range' with art-only first_line/last_line anchors to exclude the fence and prose. MSG 4, MSG 8, and MSG 10 have NO closing fence (truncated mid-figure / end of file), so their last_line is the final emitted art line. The 'first_line' anchor '╭─────────╮' is identical for MSG 8 and MSG 10 (both start the same way) — disambiguated by msg_indices. The 'last_line' anchors for MSG 8 ('├─────────') and MSG 10 ('╰────...') differ and are unique within their messages.
